candidates.reasoning: The vast, arid pampa/steppe landscape with low-lying scrub and sandy soil is highly characteristic of the Patagonian region. The road quality and fence style are common in rural Argentine provinces.

The landscape is virtually identical to the Argentine side of Patagonia, featuring the same arid plateau and sparse vegetation. Both countries share this specific geographic environment in the southern cone.
While generally greener, some interior regions exhibit similar flat, open plains, though the extreme aridity seen here is more typical of the further south/west.
The sandy soil and sparse scrub resemble the Namibian interior, though the specific vegetation and road markings are more aligned with South American standards.

evidence: Flat horizon with distant low hills

candidates.reasoning: The combination of arid scrubland, sparse xeric grasses, and low-lying thorny shrubs is highly characteristic of the Monte desert or Patagonian steppe. This vegetation pattern is very similar to that found in southern Chile, but the vast, open plains are more typical of the Argentine interior.

The arid, scrubby vegetation and sandy soil are consistent with the northern Atacama or the semi-arid regions of central Chile. The plant morphology is nearly identical to that of neighboring Argentina in these shared desert biomes.

The image shows left-hand traffic (LHT) with a yellow center line and white edge lines, which is standard for South African national roads. The arid landscape and road quality are consistent with the Karoo or Northern Cape regions.
Botswana also utilizes LHT and yellow center lines on its main roads. The infrastructure and surrounding desert environment are nearly identical to those found in South Africa.
Namibia follows LHT and uses yellow center lines. The extremely arid, flat terrain is highly characteristic of Namibian road infrastructure in remote areas.
Australia uses LHT and yellow center lines in many rural areas. However, the specific combination of road shoulder texture and vegetation is more typical of Southern Africa.

The fence posts are simple wooden stakes common in rural Argentine pampas/arid regions. The road quality and the specific 'halo' or blur pattern at the bottom of the frame are consistent with Google's coverage in South America.

The arid landscape combined with the simple wooden fence posts is very common in northern Chile. The camera generation and image quality match the regional coverage patterns seen in the Southern Cone.
While the fence style is similar, the extreme aridity is less common than in Argentina or Chile, though some interior regions may match.

evidence: Google Street View camera blur/halo at bottom

country_result:
Country: Argentina
Coordinates: -42.0, -68.0
Reasoning: The image shows a vast, extremely flat, arid steppe landscape with pale sandy-brown soil and sparse xeric scrub, which is highly characteristic of the Patagonian region of Argentina. The Regulatory agent confirmed Right-Hand Traffic (RHT) with a yellow center line, ruling out Southern African and Australian candidates. The scale of the unobstructed plains and the specific style of wooden fence posts are more indicative of the Argentine interior than the more rugged or narrow arid zones of Chile.
